data.name: Subtitle
data.value: Load a folder or a file to build the queue.

data.name: DropZoneHelp
data.value: Or click Browse to choose a folder or a file.

data.name: OutputLocationHint
data.value: The result will appear in the “{0}” folder next to the file.

data.name: OutputFolderNearFileLabel
data.value: Result folder next to the file

data.name: RenderFinishAfterCurrentLog
data.value: The current queue will stop after this file finishes.

data.name: PausedProcessExitedMessage
data.value: One of the background processes exited while rendering was paused.\nThe current file will be stopped, and the app will now try to continue it through recovery.

data.name: PausedProcessExitedAutoResumeStarting
data.value: Trying to continue the interrupted file through recovery.

data.name: PausedProcessExitedAutoResumeFailed
data.value: Could not continue the interrupted file automatically: {0}

data.name: OutputConflictMessage
data.value: This result file already exists:\n{0}\nWhat would you like to do?

data.name: ResumeUnavailableMessage
data.value: This file cannot be resumed from the saved state.\nYou can replace it or skip it.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonMissingOutput
data.value: The unfinished result file was not found next to the expected result.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonProbeUnavailable
data.value: The unfinished file could not be checked with ffprobe, so it is not safe to resume it.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonInvalidOutput
data.value: The unfinished result file is damaged or no longer readable as video, so it cannot be resumed safely.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonMissingState
data.value: No valid unfinished render state was found, so the app does not know where to continue from.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonAlreadyComplete
data.value: This file is already marked as completed, so resuming is no longer needed.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonSourceMismatch
data.value: The saved state belongs to a different source file and cannot be used for this render.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonSettingsChanged
data.value: The render settings changed after the interruption, so resuming the old file would not be safe.

data.name: ResumeUnavailableReasonGeneric
data.value: The saved state or unfinished file is not suitable for a safe resume.

data.name: ResumePreflightRecovering
data.value: Recovering the readable part of the file

data.name: BenchmarkHint
data.value: Run the benchmark to check your hardware and suggest recommended settings.

data.name: BenchmarkChartsHint
data.value: Open a separate window for speed and quality charts.

data.name: BenchmarkChartsWindowHint
data.value: Speed and quality charts at a glance.

data.name: BenchmarkSourceHint
data.value: Choose the source file for the benchmark.

data.name: BenchmarkSourceFileHint
data.value: The benchmark runs on a single video file, not a folder.

data.name: BenchmarkOutputHint
data.value: This folder stores benchmark samples and reports.

data.name: BenchmarkQualityNote
data.value: Quality uses a sampled-frame similarity score.

data.name: BenchmarkErrorUnableToCreateSampleClipWithExitCode
data.value: Unable to create benchmark sample clip. ffmpeg exit code: {0}

data.name: BenchmarkErrorUnableToCreateSampleClip
data.value: Unable to create benchmark sample clip.

data.name: AntiFlickerHint
data.value: Keeps small flicker under control without smearing lines.

data.name: AntiFlickerModeHint
data.value: Chooses how the app reduces flicker. If you're unsure, leave it on Flow Guided.

data.name: AntiFlickerModeHelpBody
data.value:
**Flow Guided** is the recommended default. In our tests it handled harder flicker better and tracked motion between frames more effectively.
**Luma Stabilizer** is the softer and simpler mode. It adds less processing cost, but its stabilization is usually weaker.
If you're unsure, keep **Flow Guided**.

data.name: AntiFlickerPresetHint
data.value: each preset includes its own mode, toggle, and strength

data.name: AntiFlickerAnimeHint
data.value: Flow Guided by default. Best for line art and subtle detail.

data.name: AntiFlickerVideoHint
data.value: Flow Guided by default. Balanced for mixed live-action footage.

data.name: AntiFlickerFacesHint
data.value: Flow Guided by default. Softens flicker while protecting faces.

data.name: AntiFlickerUltraHint
data.value: Flow Guided with higher strength for the most stubborn anime flicker. Best for maximum stability.

data.name: CustomTargetDialogPrompt
data.value: Enter the result height in pixels. Minimum is 120.

data.name: CustomTargetDialogValidation
data.value: Enter a whole number of 120 or higher.

data.name: EncoderPresetHint
data.value: Controls how hard the video encoder works after upscaling. Faster presets usually use less time.

data.name: EncoderPresetHelpBody
data.value:
**fast**: uses the least encoder time and is best for quick checks or low-power machines. **medium**: balances encoding time and file size for most jobs. **slower**: spends more time per frame and usually gives more efficient compression and a slightly smaller file.
Beyond speed, the preset changes how much effort the encoder spends on compression decisions. Slower presets often trade CPU time for smaller output and sometimes a bit more compression efficiency.

data.name: ContainerFutureLabel
data.value: Container (more options will be available later)

data.name: CodecHelpBody
data.value:
A codec defines how the video is compressed.
**x264** — faster and broadly compatible. **x265** — usually makes the result file smaller, but takes longer.

data.name: FormatHelpBody
data.value:
Format here means the result resolution.
**720p** — makes it HD. **1080p** — makes it Full HD. **1440p** — makes it QHD. **2160p** — renders 4K. **4320p** — renders 8K.

data.name: ContainerHelpBody
data.value:
A container is the outer file wrapper for the video.
Right now only **MKV** is available here. More container options will appear later.

data.name: FfmpegThreadsHelpBody
data.value:
This is the thread count for FFmpeg stages such as decoding, preparation, and encoding.
More threads can help on a strong CPU. If set too high, it can also add load without much real benefit.

data.name: UpscalerJobsHelpBody
data.value:
This field uses the format **A:B:C**. Three numbers are needed because RealESRGAN uses three internal pipeline queues during processing.
**The first number** is how many jobs are fed into the pipeline at once. **The second number** is how many jobs can stay in active processing. **The third number** is how many already processed jobs can wait for the next stage.
Examples: **1:1:1** — the safest and lightest mode. **4:4:4** — a more balanced faster mode. **8:8:8** — an aggressive mode for a strong GPU with solid VRAM headroom.
Usually all three numbers are kept equal so the pipeline stays balanced.

data.name: TileSizeHelpBody
data.value:
A tile is the square image chunk that the upscaler processes at one time.
Here, size means width and height in pixels: **128** = roughly **128x128** **256** = roughly **256x256** **1024** = roughly **1024x1024**
A larger tile is usually faster but needs more VRAM. A smaller tile is safer for weaker GPUs, but can be slower.

data.name: GpuSelectionHint
data.value: Auto picks the strongest detected GPU at startup.

data.name: StartupBenchmarkPromptThanks
data.value: Thank you for using my app. It grows thanks to your support. If you ever want to support the project, you can find that option in the help section behind the question mark at the top right.

data.name: StartupBenchmarkPromptBody
data.value: On the first start, UltraFrame AI can run a short benchmark on a One Piece sample clip, check whether this PC meets the practical minimum requirements, choose the fastest GPU, and suggest RealESRGAN settings. This may take a few minutes. If you skip it, you may run into conversion errors later. You can run this benchmark again later from Settings. Run it now?

data.name: StartupBenchmarkNewHardwareGreeting
data.value: We detected a new or changed device.

data.name: StartupBenchmarkNewHardwareBody
data.value: UltraFrame AI updated the GPU selection to the strongest available option and recommends running the short benchmark again. This helps verify the new hardware, choose the best GPU, and refresh the recommended RealESRGAN settings. You can also run this benchmark later from Settings. Run it now?

data.name: StartupBenchmarkUnavailableBody
data.value: The benchmark could not start because no usable GPU or sample clip was found.

data.name: StartupBenchmarkProgressIntro
data.value: We are checking your GPUs and tuning RealESRGAN settings on a short sample clip.

data.name: StartupBenchmarkResultsSummary
data.value: Auto-tuning finished. The benchmark used {0} and prepared recommended settings for this PC.

data.name: GeneralHelpBody
data.value: This is a quick help entry point. The full help center opens from the question mark in the top right corner.

data.name: SettingsOverviewHelpBody
data.value:
This area contains the main program settings.
General: codec, resolution, container, GPU selection, and the main processing switches. Performance: encoder preset, thread counts, and rendering speed parameters. Anti-flicker: flicker reduction and its available modes for a more stable image.

data.name: HelpCenterIntro
data.value: This window combines a quick usage guide, answers to common questions, and support information for the project.

data.name: HelpCenterHowToBody
data.value: 1. Add a video file or a folder on the main screen. 2. Open settings if you want to adjust resolution, codec, GPU, or other options. 3. If the app offers the startup benchmark, it is usually a good idea to run it. It checks your hardware and suggests recommended settings. 4. Start rendering. While rendering, the separate render window shows the current stage, preview, elapsed time, ETA, and FPS. 5. If rendering is interrupted, use “Resume render” for unfinished files when possible. 6. After the queue finishes, the app shows a summary window with per-file results.

data.name: HelpFaqQuestion1
data.value: Why is the startup benchmark useful?

data.name: HelpFaqAnswer1
data.value: It checks whether the PC is strong enough for the app, compares available GPUs, and suggests practical RealESRGAN settings for this machine.

data.name: HelpFaqQuestion2
data.value: What should I do if rendering stops unexpectedly?

data.name: HelpFaqAnswer2
data.value: If an unfinished result is still there, try the resume option. The app checks whether recovery is possible and continues from the valid part when it can.

data.name: HelpFaqAnswer3
data.value: Speed mostly depends on your GPU, available VRAM, tile size, RealESRGAN thread settings, and encoder preset. More careful or higher-quality settings usually take longer.

data.name: HelpFaqQuestion4
data.value: When should I resume, and when should I overwrite?

data.name: HelpFaqAnswer4
data.value: Resume is best after an interruption or crash when there is already an unfinished result. Overwrite is better when you changed render settings or want to start the file again from scratch.

data.name: HelpFaqQuestion5
data.value: Where can I run the benchmark again later?

data.name: HelpFaqAnswer5
data.value: You can start it again from Settings with the Benchmark button. At the end, it shows recommended settings and asks whether you want to apply them.

data.name: DonationDialogThanks
data.value: Thank you for wanting to support the app.

data.name: DonationDialogBody
data.value: Below are the suggested support methods. Feel free to use whichever one is most convenient for you.

data.name: RenderSessionResultsDonatePrefix
data.value: I would be grateful for your

data.name: StartupBenchmarkResultsWeakWarning
data.value: Your GPU looks quite weak for heavy upscaling. Rendering should still work, but expect long runtimes.

data.name: StartupBenchmarkResultsOkay
data.value: Your GPU looks healthy for this workload.

data.name: HardwareDeviceMemoryUnknown
data.value: Video memory size could not be detected

data.name: HardwareNoDevicesFoundDetail
data.value: The app could not retrieve the GPU list on this system.

data.name: HardwareGpuMissingDetail
data.value: No supported GPU was detected. The benchmark is strongly recommended because conversion may fail. Minimum: {0}. Comfortable target: {1}.

data.name: HardwareGpuStaticEnoughDetail
data.value: {0} was detected. The benchmark is still recommended for a real compatibility check. Comfortable target: {1}.

data.name: HardwareGpuStaticLowDetail
data.value: {0} looks weak for this app, so conversion may fail or run very poorly. The benchmark is strongly recommended.

data.name: HardwareGpuEnoughDetail
data.value: {0} looks usable for this app. Benchmark throughput was about {1:0.0} FPS. Comfortable target: {2}.

data.name: HardwareGpuWeakDetail
data.value: {0} works, but the benchmark only reached about {1:0.0} FPS. That is below the practical minimum. Minimum: at least {2:0.0}+ FPS and something like {3}. Comfortable target: {4}.

data.name: HardwareGpuMinimumGeneric
data.value: a discrete GPU around GeForce GTX 1050 Ti or Radeon RX 560 level

data.name: HardwareGpuComfortableGeneric
data.value: a discrete GPU around GeForce RTX 2060 / Radeon RX 6600 level or better

data.name: HardwareVramMinimumDetail
data.value: Detected about {0:0.#} GB VRAM. That should be enough for the minimum. Minimum: 4 GB. A comfortable target is {1:0.#}+ GB.

data.name: HardwareVramEnoughDetail
data.value: Detected about {0:0.#} GB VRAM. That is enough. A comfortable target is {1:0.#}+ GB.

data.name: HardwareVramLowDetail
data.value: Detected about {0:0.#} GB VRAM. That is below the minimum. Minimum: {1:0.#} GB. A comfortable target is {2:0.#}+ GB.

data.name: HardwareCpuMinimumDetail
data.value: Detected {0} logical CPU threads. That should be enough for the minimum. Minimum: 4 threads. A comfortable target is {1}+ threads.

data.name: HardwareCpuEnoughDetail
data.value: Detected {0} logical CPU threads. That is enough. A comfortable target is {1}+ threads.

data.name: HardwareCpuLowDetail
data.value: Detected {0} logical CPU threads. That is below the minimum. Minimum: {1} threads. A comfortable target is {2}+ threads.

data.name: HardwareRamMinimumDetail
data.value: Detected about {0:0.#} GB RAM. That should be enough for the minimum. Minimum: 8 GB. A comfortable target is {1:0.#}+ GB.

data.name: HardwareRamEnoughDetail
data.value: Detected about {0:0.#} GB RAM. That is enough. A comfortable target is {1:0.#}+ GB.

data.name: HardwareRamLowDetail
data.value: Detected about {0:0.#} GB RAM. That is below the minimum. Minimum: {1:0.#} GB. A comfortable target is {2:0.#}+ GB.

data.name: UpscalerBackendHint
data.value: Choose the upscaler backend. Use External for your own pipe-compatible wrapper.

data.name: RefinerBackendHint
data.value: Optional cleanup step after the upscaler and before encoding.

data.name: ExternalUpscalerHint
data.value: Use this for a user-provided wrapper that reads raw BGR24 frames from stdin and writes raw BGR24 frames to stdout.

data.name: ExternalUpscalerStableSrHint
data.value: StableSR must be installed and wrapped manually. Point this to your own pipe-compatible StableSR launcher.

data.name: ExternalUpscalerSupirHint
data.value: SUPIR must be installed and wrapped manually. Point this to your own pipe-compatible SUPIR launcher.

data.name: ExternalRefinerHint
data.value: The selected external tool will refine already upscaled frames before they are sent to the encoder.

data.name: ExternalRefinerStableSrHint
data.value: StableSR will run as an external refinement step after the built-in upscaler. Manual setup is required.

data.name: ExternalRefinerSupirHint
data.value: SUPIR will run as an experimental external refinement step after the built-in upscaler.

data.name: ExternalUpscalerHelpBody
data.value: External backends must work as pipe wrappers: read raw BGR24 frames from stdin and write raw BGR24 frames to stdout. Available placeholders in the arguments template: {width}, {height}, {frameBudget}, {channels}, {scale}, {input}, {output}, {modelDir}, {modelDirQ}, {threads}, {threadsQ}, {tileSize}, {gpuId}. Example wrapper-style template: -p -W {width} -H {height} -N {frameBudget} -c {channels} -i {input} -o {output} -s {scale} -m {modelDirQ} -j {threadsQ}

data.name: ExternalUpscalerPathRequired
data.value: External upscaler executable path is not set.

data.name: ExternalUpscalerArgumentsRequired
data.value: External upscaler arguments template is empty.

data.name: OverwriteExistingOutputHelpBody
data.value:
If enabled, an existing result file is replaced automatically.
If disabled, the app asks what to do for each conflict: skip, replace, skip all, replace all.
That choice applies only to the current render session.

data.name: PreserveIncompleteOutputHint
data.value: Leave unfinished result files in place instead of deleting them.

data.name: PreserveIncompleteOutputHelpBody
data.value: If enabled, an unfinished result file is kept when rendering stops or fails. This can help with troubleshooting or future recovery.

data.name: RepairBrokenTimestampsHint
data.value: Fix isolated broken timeline spikes from damaged sources so playback does not jump or stall later.

data.name: RepairBrokenTimestampsHelpBody
data.value:
Use this when the source file has a damaged spot with a sudden timestamp spike. The app will smooth only obvious isolated jumps so playback stays stable.
This helps with broken rips where one bad segment can throw later timing off.
Turn it off if you want to preserve the source timeline exactly as-is, including a broken jump.

data.name: DeleteAllConfirmMessage
data.value: Your source videos will stay where they are. Continue?

data.name: BackgroundColorDialogBody
data.value: Choose the global app background color. It is applied immediately to every window behind the cards.

data.name: LogPickFolderHint
data.value: Choose a folder or a video file to get started.

data.name: LogBatchFailed
data.value: The batch stopped with an error: {0}

data.name: LogSelectVideoFolder
data.value: Choose the folder or file that contains your videos.

data.name: LogSelectOutputFolder
data.value: Choose where the output should go.

data.name: CloseDuringRenderMessage
data.value: Work is still running. If you close now, the current job will be canceled and you'll need to start this file again. Exit anyway?

data.name: LogWaitingFirstUpscaledFrame
data.value: Waiting for the first result frame

data.name: LogInvalidVideoInfo
data.value: I couldn't read the video info.

data.name: LogOutputExists
data.value: Output is already there, so I'll skip it
